reviews.review_description: I was waiting a while for this game. Sadly, the launch titles are not varied enough. There is nobody playing (at least any time I launched it). No Editor (was something they advertised, it may be on it's way but it may be a little too late when it gets here.). I may be wrong but a lot of the kick starter games promised at launch are gone and there are essential games for something like this that are just not present. Bummer, I was looking forward to this. Refunded until I see further developments.

reviews.review_title: Please make a  ORIGINAL Scrabble  GAME
reviews.review_description: Please  please please can you make  a original  SCRABBLE game in all the years the quest has been out there never been a scrabble game , but to have a original scrabble board game would be amazing  ide buy the game just for that alone  , Also  another one brilliant game is a board game called   FRUSTRATION, 4 people move there way round the board clicking the dice in the middle  to get there turn ,first one home wins  these would be amazing on quest vr

I played with my brother in Canada while I was in Belgium and he selected Americas & I selected Europe. There were multiple (10 I think) disconnections throughout 2 hours of (fun) playthrough. The game is quite good, can be a bit difficult to aim and understand the way to play it (no tutorial). I would suggest having a way to write down notes or have a rulebook nearby to write down rules for some of the more complicated games (Sultan & later Terraforming Mars). I also didn't have the possibility to play in Mixed Reality (greyed out) which would have been even more awesome. I am bit concerned on pricing as it seems this game will be 20-30 euros with probably 5 standard games & having to buy licenses (unless user made) that may be 5 euros each. If that is the pricing, I could go behind it, but I hope it won't be more.

Just a demo though...
The description doesn't say that

I've been really looking forward to this! But I was hoping each game would have a way of walking you through what you're supposed to do. Kind of like how Catan is. Where it prompts you what to do or at least explains. I love board games and especially love discovering new ones. And a lot of the fun of playing them virtually is the ability to learn how to play through prompts. I wanna give it 5 stars so bad, but I had no idea how to play the game I tried.

Had a great time playing ludo with a mate in multiplayer. A few things could make this better, like some kind of sound or animation when a player wins and better, more interactive tutorials for the games. Love what's here so far :)
